prompt: I want to do amortized Bayesian inference for a simple linear regression y = alpha + beta*x + noise(sigma). The trick is that different datasets will have different sample sizes N, ranging from 10 to 200. I need the trained network to handle any N in that range. Set up the BayesFlow workflow with proper handling of the variable sample size.
assertions: Defines a meta function that returns varying N (e.g., np.random.randint(10, 200))
Uses bf.make_simulator() with meta_fn= for the variable sample size
Uses bf.Adapter() with .as_set() on the (x, y) observations
Concatenates x and y into summary_variables as a (N, 2) set
Uses .broadcast() for N to match batch dimensions
Routes N (or sqrt(N)) as inference_conditions, not through the summary network
Uses a SetTransformer as the summary network (data is exchangeable given parameters)
Calls .constrain('sigma', lower=0) for the positive noise parameter
Passes validation_data= to the fit_* call
Posterior samples use original parameter names (alpha, beta, sigma)
Demonstrates or mentions posterior predictive checks reusing the observation model function
